items.q: What makes the antigen-binding site of an antibody specific to one antigen?
items.correct: The variable region — its amino acid sequence (and thus shape) differs between antibodies, complementing a specific antigen.
items.misconception: All antibodies have the same binding site; specificity comes from where they go in the body.
items.random_wrong: Antibodies bind to any antigen they meet first.
items.why: Students miss the role of the variable region in determining specificity, thinking antibodies are interchangeable generic 'germ-killers'.

items.q: What is produced in anaerobic respiration in human muscle cells?
items.correct: Lactic acid (lactate) and a small amount of ATP — no CO2 or water in this pathway.
items.misconception: Anaerobic respiration produces ethanol and CO2 in humans.
items.random_wrong: Anaerobic respiration produces oxygen as a waste product.
items.why: Students confuse anaerobic respiration in yeast (alcohol + CO2) with the human form (lactic acid).

items.q: Why does the left ventricle have a thicker muscular wall than the right?
items.correct: It must pump blood at high pressure around the whole body; the right ventricle only pumps to the nearby lungs.
items.misconception: The left ventricle is thicker because it handles oxygenated blood, which is harder to pump.
items.random_wrong: The left ventricle is thicker because it is on the same side as the brain.
items.why: Students focus on blood type (oxygenated vs not) rather than the pressure needed to push blood around the systemic circuit.

items.q: Munch's pressure flow hypothesis for phloem transport dates from:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 1930. “1859” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
The answer examiners want is: Auxin (IAA). “Cytokinin” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Year Went demonstrated auxin in oat coleoptiles?
items.why: The real figure is 1926 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
